Command evidence shows all 24 commands at exit_code 0, including npm run closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test (2988 pass / 0 fail), npm run validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check, build, and node --test packages/migration/test/model.test.js (40 pass / 0 fail). Manifest production_ready=false and runtime_ready=false (manifest.json:6-7), and the construction-inspection/adjudication/claude-review-result artifacts are pending_review; these are the expected pre-closeout placeholders to be resolved by acceptance of exactly this one valid hardened read-only receipt, not defects, and the pack makes no runtime or enterprise-trust overclaim. CP178+ hardening is enforced: forbidden_review_evidence covers not_logged_in/malformed_json/usage_limit/tool_call_shaped_output/zero_byte_stdout/fenced_json_with_prose (registry.js:496), the current placeholder result's 0-byte stdout is correctly NOT counted (exactly_one_valid_pack_level_claude_review_run=false, valid_review_receipts=[]), and the readiness auth-preflight passed (exit 0, 1434-byte valid JSON). Changed-file-scope matches the manifest implementation refs; unrelated dirty/untracked files (closeout-pack-plan, next-pack-queue, progress-control-room, workbook docs) are preserved and not inferred into pack scope. Claude remains a read-only, non-final reviewer; this receipt enables, and does not block, CP00-768 closeout.
